t: BBQS — Brain Behavior Quantification and Synchronization
b:
A BRAIN-Initiative consortium developing tools to capture and quantify behavior at high temporal resolution and across multiple dimensions, to synchronize behavior with simultaneously-recorded brain activity, and to build conceptual and computational models of behavior. The lab runs BARD.CC, the BBQS AI Resource and Data Coordinating Center.
 NIDA U24 DA064429 (Lead PI; MPI Cabrera, Kennedy) Nader Nikbakht Satrajit S Ghosh Suliman Sharif Tek Raj Chhetri

t: DANDI — Distributed Archives for Neurophysiology Data Integration
b:
A platform for data ingestion, search, and computing for cellular neurophysiology. DANDI is the BRAIN Initiative's publishing-grade archive for neurophysiology, behavioral, and imaging data.
 NIMH R24 MH117295 (Lead PI; MPI Halchenko, Dartmouth) Kabilar Gunalan Nima Dehghani Satrajit S Ghosh Christopher J Markiewicz

t: BICAN Knowledgebase (BrainKB)
b:
An extensible, open-access Brain Cell Knowledge Base built for the BRAIN Initiative Cell Atlas Network. Ingests and standardizes cell-type information across multimodal, multi-species brain atlases and exposes it via the BrainKB platform, the bkbit toolkit, and LinkML-authored schemas. The lab maintains BrainKB, the StructSense extraction agent, and the schemas / data translators.
 NIMH U24 MH130918 (MPI with Mufti, Hawrylycz, Ng — Allen Institute) Dorota Jarecka Nader Nikbakht Nima Dehghani Puja Trivedi Satrajit S Ghosh Suliman Sharif Tek Raj Chhetri Jai Amin

t: BICAN UM1 — Multi-Scale Multi-Omic Human Brain Atlas
b:
A BRAIN Initiative UM1 center for multi-scale, multi-omic atlasing of the human brain, contributing analytics and platform integration for proteomic imaging and related modalities. The flagship consortium output to date is Park et al. (2024) in Science, an integrated multiscale molecular imaging and phenotyping platform for the human brain.
 NIMH UM1 MH134812 (Co-I) Kabilar Gunalan Satrajit S Ghosh

t: LINC / CONNECTS — Large-scale Imaging of Neural Circuits
b:
A multi-site center combining macaque and human dMRI (660 mT/m and 500 mT/m), light-sheet microscopy with tracers, polarization-sensitive OCT, HiP-CT, and micro-CT into integrated pipelines for clearing, imaging, storage, and dissemination of neural-circuit data. Use cases include DBS-circuit mapping and biophysical modeling.
 NINDS UM1 NS132358 (Site PI) Kabilar Gunalan Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Bridge2AI Voice
b:
Build an ethically-sourced, diverse, AI-ready database of 10 000 human voices linked to health information across five disease categories: cardio-respiratory, mood, pediatric speech, voice, and neurological disorders. The consortium develops standards for acoustic research data, technical infrastructure for collection / storage / sharing, ethical guidelines, and educational dissemination. The lab maintains b2aiprep (data packaging) and contributes to the protocol design, ethics, and pediatric arm.
 NIH OD OT2 OD032720 (MPI) Jordan Wilke Kaley Jenney Rahul Brito Satrajit S Ghosh Daniel M. Low Fabio Catania

t: senselab
b:
Research software toolkit for voice / speech / behavioral analysis and biomarker identification. "Streamlines, optimizes, and enforces best open-science practices for processing and analyzing behavioral data (primarily voice and speech, but also text and video) using robust, reproducible pipelines and utilities." Used by the Bridge2AI-Voice and Riverst / KIVA stacks.
 Cross-funded by the Child Mind Institute, NIH Bridge2AI-Voice (OT2 OD032720), the Simons Foundation (SAILS), the MIT Generative AI Consortium (Riverst / KIVA), the ICON Center at the McGovern Institute (MIT), and the ReadNet project.
 Jordan Wilke Satrajit S Ghosh Bruke Wossenseged Fabio Catania Gasser Elbanna Isaac Bevers Miles Silva

t: Riverst / KIVA — Digital tutor for childhood literacy
b:
Riverst is a digital-tutor platform for childhood literacy; KIVA is an instance focused on vocabulary learning. Built on Pipecat and senselab, integrating speech-feature extraction. Research questions: Can an avatar understand child speech? Can LLMs adapt to each child? Can an LLM effectively teach vocabulary?
 MGAIC (MIT Generative AI Consortium), 2025–2026 Jordan Wilke Satrajit S Ghosh Fabio Catania

t: ReproNim — Center for Reproducible Neuroimaging Computation
b:
A Center dedicated to improving reproducibility in neuroimaging via best practices, tools, and training. Current efforts include Simple 2 (re-executable neuroimaging publications with NIDM provenance), ReproSchema (standardized questionnaires / assessments for ABCD, HBCD, Bridge2AI), ReproBRAISE / ABCD-ReproNim (Reproducible BRAIn State Extraction), containerized BIDS apps (FreeSurfer / FSL / ANTs / MRIQC), and BABS (BIDS App Bootstrap).
 NIBIB P41 EB019936 (Site PI; PI Kennedy, UMass Med; Director TR&D Project 2) Dorota Jarecka Satrajit S Ghosh Yibei Chen Christopher J Markiewicz Daniel M. Low Isaac Bevers Jakub Kaczmarzyk Mathias Goncalves Sanu Ann Abraham Smruti Padhy

t: Nipype / Pydra
b:
Nipype is a community-developed Python project providing a uniform interface to existing neuroimaging software (ANTS, SPM, FSL, FreeSurfer, Camino, MRtrix, MNE, AFNI, Slicer) and underpinning many popular pipelines including fmriprep. Pydra is a lightweight Python dataflow engine designed to succeed Nipype: reproducible, scalable workflows linking shell commands and Python functions.
 NIBIB R01 EB020740 (PI, 2016–2020; project ongoing as community software) Dorota Jarecka Satrajit S Ghosh Christopher J Markiewicz Jakub Kaczmarzyk Mathias Goncalves

t: SAILS — Speech & Action In-home Learning System
b:
Into the Wild — automated quantification of autism-relevant behaviors from naturalistic home videos. The study pipeline runs end-to-end: parents are reached through SPARK Research Match, submit questionnaires and home-video uploads, and complete a remote language assessment; videos pass automated metadata checks and human quality review; trained annotators produce ground-truth labels against a shared manual with ICC reliability checks; and VLM-based models score uniform frame samples for video-level behavior predictions. Brings together three theses: Manaal Mohammed's on target-child behavior detection (vocalizations via USC-SAIL / Whisper baseline; locomotion via S3D gated 3D CNN), Lucie Bierent's on socialization-behavior recognition (response to name, non-verbal communication, gesture prediction), and Bruke Wossenseged's thesis work.
 Simons Foundation (Site PI, 2025–2026); Kennedy Krieger Institute partnership Satrajit S Ghosh Yibei Chen Manaal Mohammed Aparna BG Bruke Wossenseged Lucie Bierent Fabio Catania

t: Brain States
b:
A brain state is a latent (hidden) variable representing a recurring whole-brain configuration of neural activity — characterized by emissions (spatial patterns), transition probabilities, and dwell time. Research questions: Is there systematic structure in how brain configurations relate to the world and behavior? How does this structure differ between the neurodivergent and neurotypical populations?
  Satrajit S Ghosh Yibei Chen

t: Psychotic Experiences in ABCD
b:
Analyses of psychotic experiences in the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study — risk factors, transitions, and adolescent brain-imaging correlates. # TODO expand description
  Debbie Burdinski Satrajit S Ghosh Alisha Kodibagkar

t: BANDA — Boston Adolescent Neuroimaging of Depression and Anxiety
b:
A Human Connectome Project arm (Connectomes Related to Human Diseases) collecting multimodal MRI, clinical, and neuropsychological data from 215 adolescents aged 14–17, 152 of whom had current anxiety and/or depressive disorders at intake. Imaging spans structural (T1/T2), resting-state and task fMRI, and diffusion-weighted scans, released as both unprocessed and HCP minimally-preprocessed data. The lab contributed the imaging pipelines, quality assurance, and data-release engineering, and continues to support connectome-based predictive-modeling analyses of depression and anxiety risk across BANDA and ABCD.
 NIMH — Connectomes Related to Human Diseases (Human Connectome Project) Satrajit S Ghosh Mathias Goncalves

t: ReadNet — ML for verbal literacy screening
b:
Manual scoring of verbal literacy screeners is time-consuming and unreliable; ReadNet is an ML approach to help teachers quickly and reliably identify children needing early-literacy intervention. The project ran the DrivenData "Goodnight Moon, Hello Early Literacy Screening" public competition in 2024–2025 (winning AUROC 0.97 vs Whisper baseline 0.59), with a voice-cloning anonymization scheme for data release.
 Harvard University gift (Schmidt Futures & Citadel; Site Co-I) + MIT GenAI Consortium Meral Hacikamiloglu Satrajit S Ghosh Fabio Catania

t: HypSyNth — AI co-scientist for neurophysiology
b:
An "AI co-scientist" loop for neurophysiology: metadata / data ingestion → hypothesis refinement → in-silico experiment design → analysis → repeat, applied to large-scale neurophysiology archives. # TODO expand description with methods + outputs
 MGAIC (MIT Generative AI Consortium) Nima Dehghani Satrajit S Ghosh

t: StructSense — Agentic structured information extraction
b:
A task-agnostic multi-agent system for structured information extraction. Used by BrainKB to extract causal mechanisms (not just entities) from neuroscience literature, with a prototype tool that converts flat NER data into valid, Marr-aligned JSON-LD. arXiv 2507.03674 (Chhetri, Chen, Trivedi, Jarecka et al., 2025).
  Dorota Jarecka Nader Nikbakht Puja Trivedi Satrajit S Ghosh Suliman Sharif Tek Raj Chhetri Yibei Chen

t: Abstract Atlas — browseable embedding map of conference abstracts and PubMed
b:
A static, browser-side atlas for searching and exploring the neuroscience literature landscape via embedding-space visualisation. Pulls OHBM 2026 abstracts (Oxford Abstracts GraphQL API) and a NeuroScape PubMed corpus, enriches each record with figure analysis and OpenAlex reference matching, generates embeddings (Voyage, MiniLM-L6, OpenAI, PubMedBERT, sentence-transformers), reduces with UMAP, and serves the result as a SvelteKit site with lexical search, semantic search (transformers.js, MiniLM-ONNX in-browser), faceted filters, and dual semantic-cluster lenses. The lab uses it to map OHBM 2026 against the wider PubMed neuroimaging atlas.
 Lab infrastructure (no external grant) Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Nobrainer — Neural network tool suite for imagers
b:
Open Python library to simplify integrating deep learning into neuroimaging research, distributing user-friendly cloud-enabled end-user applications for the community. The Nobrainer / KWYK framework underlies several brain-segmentation and meningioma-detection studies.
 NIMH RF1 MH121885 (PI, 2020–2024)  Aakanksha Rana Jakub Kaczmarzyk Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Mumble Melody — Musically-modulated auditory feedback for people who stutter
b:
Mobile app and one-month clinical study using altered auditory feedback (AAF) — pitch shifts, delays, reverberation, whisper, harmony — to increase fluency for people who stutter. US Patent 11,727,949 B2 issued Aug 2023 for the underlying technique.
 McGovern Institute NTP + MIT Deshpande Center (PI; co-PI Machover)  Akito van Troyer Alisha Kodibagkar George Stefanakis Megha Vemuri Mike Erkkinen Rebecca Kleinberger Satrajit S Ghosh Tod Machover

t: Naturalistic Parcellation and audiovisual integration
b:
A Neuroscout project generating cortical parcellations from naturalistic (movie-viewing) neuroimaging datasets, with goals including character-ID decoding, audiovisual-feature mapping, auditory-specific and whole-brain parcellations, and reproducibility across datasets.
 NIMH R01 MH109682 (Site PI; PI Yarkoni)  Christopher J Markiewicz Jeff Mentch Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Detecting vocal-fold paralysis from voice
b:
Analyzed sustained vowel and read speech to detect unilateral vocal-fold paralysis; explored biases in recording and compared ML performance to clinicians' diagnosis-from-voice ability.
   Daniel M. Low Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Systematic review of speech in psychiatric disorders
b:
Systematic review of 127 studies across 8 psychiatric disorders; catalogued acoustic features and offered guidelines for data acquisition and machine-learning models.
   Daniel M. Low Kate Bentley Satrajit S Ghosh

t: COVID-19 mental-health concerns on Reddit
b:
Analyzed 800 000+ posts from 2018–2021 to track semantic-content changes across 15 mental-health support communities, surfacing vulnerable groups and a doubling of posts within suicidality and loneliness clusters. Released a Mental Health Reddit Dataset.
   Daniel M. Low Satrajit S Ghosh

t: Peer responses to suicidal posts among veterans
b:
Estimated the effect of peer responses to suicidal posts among U.S. Servicemembers and veterans, using observational data and a deep learning approach on social-media interactions.
